description: Canonical known-good test vectors for the AgentGraph partner webhook HMAC contract. Use these to unit-test your receiver before wiring live traffic. See webhook-contract.md for the full spec.

canonical_serialization.note: HMAC is computed over the exact raw body bytes as sent on the wire. Python: json.dumps(body, separators=(',', ':'), sort_keys=True).encode('utf-8'). The JWS value in the body is treated as an opaque string for the purposes of this test — signature placeholders are fine.

vectors.name: score_increase
vectors.description: A repo's score rose from 71 to 82. Both old_score and new_score present.

vectors.name: first_scan_null_old_score
vectors.description: First time this repo was scanned — old_score is explicitly null.

reproduce_in_python:
import hmac, hashlib, json
secret = 'moltbridge-shared-secret-example-2026'
body = {'type':'scan-change','repo':'agentgraph-co/agentgraph','new_score':82,'old_score':71,'changed_at':'2026-04-20T22:00:00+00:00','jws':'eyJhbGciOiJFZERTQSIsInR5cCI6IkpXUyJ9..SIGNATURE_PLACEHOLDER'}
raw = json.dumps(body, separators=(',', ':'), sort_keys=True).encode('utf-8')
print('sha256=' + hmac.new(secret.encode('utf-8'), raw, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest())
